36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.

41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54

58	# Lean Auditor Directive

68

71	```

73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.

79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.

131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)

48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.

79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. 81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. 83	## Protected Declarations

89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.

91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. 102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.

The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. 23	flatness record. The general implication "`M` tensor-invertible `⟹` `M` locally
24	free of rank one" (the converse spreading-out, Stacks Lemma 0B8M when stalks are
25	local) is *not* crossed here: the carrier is already locally trivial, so the
26	finite-presentation spreading-out is never needed.

121	/-! ## §1. From pointwise triviality to a trivialising cover -/
122	
123	/-- **Trivialising affine cover** (Stacks 01CS / Definition 17.25.1).
124	A locally trivial module `M` admits an indexed trivialising affine cover: an
125	index type `I`, a family of affine opens `U i` covering `X`, and for each `i` a
126	`𝒪_{U_i}`-module isomorphism `M|_{U_i} ≅ 𝒪_{U_i}`.
127	
128	The witness repackages the pointwise existential `IsLocallyTrivial M` (take
129	`I := X` and assign each point its chosen neighbourhood). -/
130	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover {M : X.Modules}
131	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
132	    ∃ (I : Type u) (U : I → X.Opens),
133	      (∀ i, IsAffineOpen (U i)) ∧ iSup U = ⊤ ∧
134	        ∀ i, Nonempty (M.restrict (U i).ι ≅
135	          SheafOfModules.unit (U i : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
136	  refine ⟨X, fun x => (hM x).choose, fun x => (hM x).choose_spec.2.1, ?_,
137	    fun x => (hM x).choose_spec.2.2⟩
138	  rw [eq_top_iff]
139	  intro x _
140	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
141	  exact ⟨x, (hM x).choose_spec.1⟩
142	
143	/-! ## §1b. The canonical finite presentation of the unit module -/
144	
145	section UnitPresentation
146	
147	universe u₁ v₁
148	
149	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
150	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
151	  [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
152	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
153	
154	/-- The canonical isomorphism `free PUnit ≅ unit R`: the free module on a single
155	generator is the unit module. -/
156	noncomputable def freeUnitIso :
157	    SheafOfModules.free.{u} (R := R) PUnit.{u + 1} ≅ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
158	  Limits.coproductUniqueIso (fun (_ : PUnit.{u + 1}) ↦ SheafOfModules.unit R)
159	
160	/-- The canonical generating sections of `unit R`: one generator (the image of
161	the free generator under `freeUnitIso`). -/
162	noncomputable def unitGenerators : (SheafOfModules.unit R).GeneratingSections where
163	  I := PUnit.{u + 1}
164	  s := (SheafOfModules.unit R).freeHomEquiv freeUnitIso.hom
165	  epi := by
166	    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
167	    infer_instance
168	
169	/-- The canonical finite presentation of the unit module `unit R`: one generator,
170	no relations (its generating map is an isomorphism, so the relation kernel is the
171	zero object). -/
172	noncomputable def unitPresentation : (SheafOfModules.unit R).Presentation where
173	  generators := unitGenerators
174	  relations :=
175	    { I := PEmpty.{u + 1}
176	      s := PEmpty.elim
177	      epi := by
178	        have hiso : IsIso (unitGenerators (R := R)).π := by
179	          dsimp only [unitGenerators, SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.π]
180	          rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
181	          infer_instance
182	        have : Mono (unitGenerators (R := R)).π := inferInstance
183	        have hz : Limits.IsZero (Limits.kernel (unitGenerators (R := R)).π) :=
184	          Limits.isZero_kernel_of_mono _
185	        exact ⟨fun g h _ => hz.eq_of_src g h⟩ }
186	
187	instance : (unitPresentation (R := R)).IsFinite where
188	  isFiniteType_generators := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite PUnit.{u + 1})⟩
189	  isFiniteType_relations := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite PEmpty.{u + 1})⟩
190	
191	end UnitPresentation
192	
193	/-! ## §2. The trivial presentation on a chart -/
194	
195	/-- **The over↔restrict trivialisation bridge.** Transports the scheme-level
196	trivialisation `e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U` (an isomorphism in
197	`SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf`, the category of modules on the *open
198	subscheme* `U`) to a slice-level trivialisation
199	`M.over U ≅ 𝒪_U` (in `SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`, the category of
200	modules on the *slice site* `J.over U`).
201	
202	The two source categories are equivalent — the open-immersion functor
203	`U.ι.opensFunctor : Opens ↥U ≌ {V ∈ Opens X | V ≤ U} = Over U` induces an
204	equivalence `SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf ≌ SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`
205	identifying `M.restrict U.ι ↦ M.over U` and `unit ↦ unit`. That modules-level
206	lift of `Opens.overEquivalence` is the shared root `Scheme.Modules.overEquivalence`
207	of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SheafOverEquivalence`, and the bridge is the general
208	`Scheme.Modules.chartOverIso` built there as the three-step composite
209	`(restrictOverIso).symm ≪≫ overEquivalence.functor.mapIso e ≪≫ unitOverIso`.
210	
211	This local declaration now **redirects** to that general construction (the
212	iter-258 shared-root pivot), so it carries no `sorry` of its own; the remaining
213	mathematical content (the three isos `overEquivalence` / `restrictOverIso` /
214	`unitOverIso`) lives in the shared-root file where the dual lane consumes it too.
215	Everything else in this engine (`chartPresentation`, `isFinitePresentation`,
216	`isFiniteType`) was already complete modulo this isomorphism. -/
217	noncomputable def chartOverIso (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
218	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
219	    M.over U ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) :=
220	  Scheme.Modules.chartOverIso U M e
221	
222	/-- **Finite free presentation on a chart** (Stacks, "Modules of finite
223	presentation"). On a trivialising chart `U` — i.e. with an isomorphism
224	`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` — the restriction `M.over U` admits the trivial finite free
225	presentation (one generator, no relations), obtained by transporting the
226	canonical presentation of `SheafOfModules.unit` along the trivialisation
227	(through the `chartOverIso` bridge). -/
228	noncomputable def IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
229	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
230	    (M.over U).Presentation := by
231	  exact SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
232	    (M := SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) (N := SheafOfModules.over M U)
233	    (chartOverIso M U e).inv (unitPresentation (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U))
234	
235	instance (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
236	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
237	    (IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M U e).IsFinite := by
238	  dsimp only [IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation]
239	  infer_instance
240	
241	/-! ## §3. Finite presentation of a locally trivial line bundle -/
242	
243	/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finitely presented** (Stacks 0B8M / Lemma 17.25.4).
244	A locally trivial module on a scheme is finitely presented: assemble a
245	`QuasicoherentData` from the trivialising affine cover
246	(`exists_trivializing_cover`), whose presentation at each index is the finite
247	free presentation of `chartPresentation`, and feed it to
248	`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`. No stalk computation or
249	neighbourhood-spreading is involved. -/
250	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation {M : X.Modules}
251	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
252	    M.IsFinitePresentation := by
253	  obtain ⟨I, U, hUaff, hUtop, hUiso⟩ := hM.exists_trivializing_cover
254	  -- assemble the quasi-coherent data, one finite chart presentation per index;
255	  -- `shrink` lands the index type in the site-object universe demanded by
256	  -- `IsFinitePresentation`'s existential.
257	  let q : M.QuasicoherentData :=
258	    { I := I
259	      X := U
260	      coversTop := by
261	        -- the trivialising affine opens cover the terminal object of the site
262	        intro W x hxW
263	        have hx : x ∈ iSup U := by rw [hUtop]; trivial
264	        rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup] at hx
265	        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hx
266	        exact ⟨U i ⊓ W, homOfLE inf_le_right, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_left⟩⟩, hi, hxW⟩
267	      presentation := fun i => IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M (U i) (hUiso i).some }
268	  have hsh : q.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by
269	    apply SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation.mk
270	    intro i
271	    exact inferInstanceAs (IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M _ _).IsFinite
272	  exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q.shrink, hsh⟩ }
273	
274	/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finite type and quasi-coherent** (corollary of
275	`isFinitePresentation`). Finite presentation implies finite type by Mathlib's
276	`SheafOfModules.instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation` and quasi-coherence by
277	`SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation`. -/
278	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType {M : X.Modules}
279	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
280	    M.IsFiniteType := by
281	  haveI := hM.isFinitePresentation
282	  infer_instance
283	
284	/-! ## §4. Rank one and flatness record -/
285	
286	/-- **Chart-local rank one and flatness** (Stacks, "Locally free sheaves":
287	finite locally free of rank `r`). On a trivialising chart the restriction
288	`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` is free of rank one over `𝒪_U`; in particular flat (`𝒪_U` is a flat
289	module over itself, and flatness is preserved by the isomorphism). The fact is
290	recorded chart-locally — as the rank-one free model `M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U = ⊕_{*} 𝒪_U`
291	the Quot embedding consumes — because Mathlib has no `SheafOfModules`-level
292	locally-free / flat / rank predicate. -/
293	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one {M : X.Modules}
294	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) (x : X) :
295	    ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
296	      Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
297	  exact hM x
298	
299	end LineBundle
300	
301	end Scheme

62	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
63	
64	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
65	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
66	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
67	
68	namespace PresheafOfModules
69	
70	open InternalHom Opposite
71	
72	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
73	
74	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
75	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
76	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
77	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
78	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
79	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
80	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
81	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
82	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
83	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
84	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
85	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
86	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
87	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
89	  exact
90	    { toFun := fun φ =>
91	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
92	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
93	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
94	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
95	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
96	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
97	      invFun := fun r =>
98	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
99	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
100	      left_inv := fun φ => by
101	        ext Y
102	        dsimp only
103	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
104	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
105	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
106	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
107	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
108	        rfl
109	      right_inv := fun r => by
110	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
111	            (restr X.unop
112	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
113	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
114	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
115	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
116	        exact mul_one r }
117	
118	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
119	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
120	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
121	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
122	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
123	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
124	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
125	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
126	    (fun {X Y} f => by
127	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
128	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
129	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual

437	open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
438	/-- **The local section of the hom-sheaf manufactured from `f i`** (the load-bearing piece
439	of `homOfLocalCompat`, blueprint `localSection`).  Working with the underlying `Ab`-presheaves
440	`F = M.val.presheaf`, `G = N.val.presheaf`, the presheaf of types
441	`presheafHom F G` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) sends an open `W` to the morphisms of
442	the restrictions of `F`, `G` to the slice `Over W`.  Its value at `U i` is built from the
443	components of `f i`, conjugated by `eqToHom` along the down-set identity
444	`(U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` (valid for `V ≤ U i`).  The naturality field — the genuine
445	coherence risk — is automatic on the thin poset `Opens X` once the `eqToHom`-conjugation is
446	peeled, via `Subsingleton.elim` on the hom-sets. -/
447	noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
448	    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
449	    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
450	  app W :=
451	    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
452	    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
453	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
454	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
455	    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
456	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
457	      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
458	  naturality := by
459	    intro A B φ
460	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
461	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
462	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
463	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
464	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
465	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
466	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
467	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
468	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop B).hom.le
469	    -- naturality of the underlying ab-presheaf morphism of `f i`
470	    have hm := ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).naturality κ.op
471	    -- the two thin-poset square edges agree (`Opens X` is a thin poset)
472	    have hsubM : ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm)
473	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm) ≫ ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op :=
474	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
475	    have hsubN : ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB)
476	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA) ≫ ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op :=
477	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
478	    -- M-side: the φ-restriction followed by the `eqToHom` is the `eqToHom` followed by `κ`
479	    have hML : M.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫
480	          M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm))
481	        = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm)) ≫
482	          (M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op := by
483	      rw [(M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]
484	      exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
485	    -- N-side analogue
486	    have hNR : N.val.presheaf.map ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫
487	          N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB))
488	        = N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA)) ≫
489	          N.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op := by
490	      rw [(N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubN]
491	      exact N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
492	    dsimp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.op_map, Functor.op_obj, Functor.comp_obj]
493	    rw [← Category.assoc, hML]
494	    erw [Category.assoc, reassoc_of% hm, hNR]
495	    simp only [Category.assoc]
496	    rfl

499	/-- **Convert a section of `presheafHom F G` over the terminal open `⊤` into a global
500	morphism `F ⟶ G`.**  Since `⊤` is terminal in `Opens X`, the value of `presheafHom F G`
501	at `op ⊤` already determines a full compatible family of sections (each open's value is the
502	restriction of the top section), which `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` identifies with a morphism
503	`F ⟶ G`.  This is sub-step (b) of `homOfLocalCompat`. -/
504	noncomputable def topSectionToHom {X : TopCat.{u}}
505	    {F G : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}}
506	    (s : (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).obj (op ⊤)) : F ⟶ G :=
507	  CategoryTheory.presheafHomSectionsEquiv F G
508	    ⟨fun W => (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).map (homOfLE le_top).op s, by
509	      intro W W' e
510	      dsimp only
511	      rw [← Functor.map_comp_apply]
512	      congr 1⟩

514	open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
515	/-- **Sectionwise value of `topSectionToHom`.**  At an open `W`, the recovered morphism
516	evaluates to the `Over.mk (homOfLE le_top)`-component of the top section `s`. -/
517	lemma topSectionToHom_app {X : TopCat.{u}}
518	    {F G : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}}
519	    (s : (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).obj (op ⊤)) (W : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) :
520	    (topSectionToHom s).app W = s.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE (le_top) : W.unop ⟶ ⊤))) := by
521	  obtain ⟨W⟩ := W
522	  exact CategoryTheory.presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id (homOfLE le_top) s

525	/-- **Down-set image identity.**  For `V ≤ W` (opens of a scheme `X`), the image under the
526	open immersion `W.ι` of the preimage of `V` is `V` again: `W.ι ''ᵁ (W.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V`.  This is
527	the equality powering the `eqToHom`-conjugations in `homLocalSection`. -/
528	lemma image_preimage_of_le {X : Scheme.{u}} (W : X.Opens) {V : X.Opens} (hV : V ≤ W) :
529	    W.ι ''ᵁ (W.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V := by
530	  simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
531	  exact inf_eq_right.mpr hV

535	/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**
536	
537	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
538	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
539	morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
540	that the underlying section maps of `f i` and `f j` agree, *sectionwise*, on every open
541	`V ≤ U i ⊓ U j` (each conjugated into the fixed abelian-group hom-type `M(V) ⟶ N(V)` by the
542	canonical `eqToHom`s from the down-set identity `ι(ι⁻¹V) = V`), then there is a unique global
543	morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.
544	
545	The compatibility hypothesis `hf` is the **sectionwise** overlap-agreement (iter-254 re-sign;
546	this `def` is NOT in `archon-protected.yaml` and has no compiling caller, so the prover owns its
547	signature).  The earlier `HEq` form — comparing the two `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images of
548	`f i`, `f j` along the two slice-restrictions — was *unsatisfiable*: those images live in
549	sheafifications of pullback presheaves along *different* morphisms, hence in only-isomorphic
550	(not propositionally equal) objects, so no `HEq`-elimination applies and no caller can produce
551	the datum.  The sectionwise form compares only the section maps, which live in the fixed group
552	`M(V) ⟶ N(V)`, and is exactly the data a caller (two local morphisms literally agreeing on
553	overlaps) has at hand.  See blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` sub-step (a).
